contradictions.claim: Reducing net carbon intensity by 30% by 2035
contradictions.evidence_text: Shell targets a 20% net carbon intensity reduction by 2035, subject to prevailing market conditions and the regulatory environment. This target may be revised if material changes to the business environment occur.

The Company's ability to achieve net zero emissions by 2050 or earlier is contingent upon significant technological advances not yet commercially available and supportive policy frameworks that cannot be assured.

contradictions.claim: Powering Progress: energy access for 1.4 billion people
contradictions.evidence_text: Shell cuts renewable energy investment by 20% citing poor returns, redirecting capital to oil and gas production

contradictions.claim: $2–3 billion annual investment in low-carbon energy
contradictions.evidence_text: Capital expenditure on renewables and energy solutions: $1.5 billion (2023), down from $2.1 billion (2022). Oil and gas capex increased to $20.4 billion.

contradictions.evidence_source: Shell DE Page vs Shell US Page
contradictions.evidence_text: US page states binding 30% reduction; DE page qualifies all targets as subject to market conditions — explicit hedge absent from US consumer-facing page

sec_filing.sec_language: Targeting 20% net carbon intensity reduction subject to prevailing market conditions and the regulatory environment. This target may be revised.
sec_filing.delta_description: 10 percentage point discrepancy between public marketing claims (30%) and SEC regulatory filing (20%). Filing includes material uncertainty clause absent from all public messaging.

news_violations.headline: Shell loses Dutch court case ordering 45% emissions cut by 2030
news_violations.summary: Hague district court ordered Shell to cut CO2 emissions by 45% by end of 2030 compared with 2019 levels — more aggressive than company's own targets

news_violations.headline: UK advertising watchdog bans Shell ads for misleading green claims
news_violations.summary: ASA ruled Shell's 'Drive Carbon Neutral' and similar ads were misleading because they omitted the majority of Shell's products are fossil fuels

news_violations.headline: Shell ordered to pay €17.3m for breaching Dutch emissions requirements
news_violations.summary: Dutch authorities levied fine for failure to comply with binding emissions reduction orders from 2021 court ruling

sample_reviews: They talk about sustainability in all-hands meetings and press releases, but we're told internally not to slow down production targets.
The green messaging is primarily a marketing exercise. Real operational decisions prioritize margins.
Senior leadership openly says ESG commitments are for investor relations, not actual policy.
Sustainability team has tiny budget vs communications team. That tells you everything.
